common_name: Dragon's Tongue Cucumber (Dragon Tongue, Dutch Yellow Cucumber)

planting.note: Start indoors 3–4 weeks before last frost or direct sow after last frost when soil is above 65°F; harvest while fruits are cream with purple stripes — they become fully yellow and bitter if left on the vine too long.

directions: Direct sow or transplant to well-amended, warm soil in full sun after last frost; Dragon's Tongue is a strikingly beautiful Dutch heirloom cucumber producing medium-sized (5–6 inch) cucumbers with creamy-white skin dramatically streaked and splashed with purple-violet, resulting in an ornamental appearance unlike any standard green cucumber. Harvest when fruits are creamy-white with purple markings and firm to the touch — as they continue to mature they turn yellow then bitter. The flavor when harvested at prime ripeness is mild, crisp, and slightly sweet, excellent for fresh eating and pickling. A productive and prolific vining type that does not require trellis but benefits from it.

common_name: Crystal Lemon Cucumber (Lemon Cucumber, Crystal Apple)

planting.note: Start indoors 3–4 weeks before last frost or direct sow after last frost; harvest when fruits are pale yellow-ivory and 2–3 inches in diameter — they become seedy and bitter if allowed to ripen further to full yellow.

directions: Direct sow or transplant to well-drained, warm, enriched soil in full sun after last frost; Crystal Lemon cucumber (sometimes called Crystal Apple) produces round to oval fruits roughly the size and color of a lemon at harvest — pale ivory to pale yellow, 2–3 inches in diameter, with a very mild, sweet, almost non-existent bitterness and a thin skin that does not need peeling. One of the most productive and heat-tolerant cucumbers available, and excellent for gardeners who find regular cucumbers too bitter or those who want a conversation-starting variety. Fruits should be harvested while still pale ivory-yellow; fully yellow fruits are over-ripe and seedy. Very popular with children for its unusual round shape.
